# Spec — 2026-05-19

## Executive Summary

- **3 of 5 items from 2026-05-18 are now resolved** in our codebase: MCP tool call timeout (`src/mcp/client.ts` with `MCP_TOOL_TIMEOUT` env var and per-server config), `--worktree` CLI flag (`src/cli/commands/interactive.ts`, `src/cli/commands/agent.ts`, `src/utils/gitWorktree.ts`), and compaction instruction preservation (`SUMMARY_PROMPT` at `src/compaction/index.ts:115-120` explicitly preserves user preferences/constraints/API keys)
- **2 items remain open**: Goal-driven autonomous loop (`/goal` command) and Rewind/Partial Summarize ("Summarize up to here")
- **anthropics/claude-code v2.1.144** (May 19): Major release — `/resume` for background sessions, MCP paginated `tools/list` fix (servers returning multiple pages were silently dropping tools), reactive compaction improvement (first summarize seeds from overflow size), startup timeout (side-channel API calls now time out at 15s to avoid 75s hangs on unreachable networks), file-extension mismatch fallback, fewer spurious search tool errors
- **anthropics/claude-code v2.1.143** (May 15-18): Plugin dependency enforcement, `worktree.bgIsolation: "none"` setting, stop hook block cap after 8 consecutive blocks, `/goal` evaluator fix (fires only after subagents complete)
- **cline/cline v3.0.4-v3.0.7** (May 16-19): Plugin tools/commands hydration in settings, ChatGPT OAuth model catalog, light theme TUI color improvements, agent usage reset at start of each `execute()` call, multi-agent war room demo, PR dashboard code review bot example
- **Kilo-Org/kilocode** (May 16-18): Auto-resume network reconnects (new feature), link-checker-safe network probe, REVIEW.md reviewbot guidance, JetBrains cache bypass, forbidden-string check extraction
- **aider-ai/aider**: No new commits since May 16 (bash tree-sitter tags already noted)
- **continuedev/continue**: Still dormant — last commit April 17
- **Key new gap**: MCP paginated tools/list — our `listTools()` calls at `src/mcp/client.ts:91` and `:335` do not handle pagination; MCP servers with many tools (>100) will silently lose tools beyond the first page
- **Key new gap**: Startup/API timeout guard — our provider layer has no explicit timeout on initial API connectivity checks; if the API host is unreachable (captive portal, firewall), the session could hang indefinitely

## What to build (prioritized)

### 1. MCP Paginated tools/list Support
- **What**: Handle `nextCursor` in MCP `tools/list` responses by looping until no cursor is returned, aggregating all pages of tools
- **Why**: Claude Code v2.1.144 fixed "MCP servers with paginated `tools/list` responses only returning the first page, silently dropping tools". Our `listTools()` at `src/mcp/client.ts:91` and `:335` calls `connection.client.listTools()` once without passing or checking a cursor. MCP servers with large tool catalogs (plugin registries, enterprise tool hubs) will silently lose tools beyond the first page, causing "tool not found" errors at runtime with no indication of the root cause
- **Source**: anthropics/claude-code v2.1.144 "Fixed MCP servers with paginated `tools/list` responses only returning the first page, silently dropping tools"
- **Category**: mcp
- **Size**: small (< 4h)

### 2. Goal-Driven Autonomous Loop Command
- **What**: Add a `/goal` command that accepts a completion condition (natural language or regex predicate), then iterates autonomously across turns until the condition is met. Show elapsed time, turn count, and token usage. Allow cancellation with Ctrl+C/Esc
- **Why**: Claude Code v2.1.139 shipped `/goal` and v2.1.143 refined its evaluator (doesn't fire while subagents are running). Our orchestrator handles single turns and expects the user to manually re-prompt. For batch tasks (fix all lint errors, migrate all files, run tests until green), the user must babysit each iteration. A `/goal` command automates multi-turn convergence toward a defined condition
- **Source**: anthropics/claude-code v2.1.139 `/goal` command, v2.1.143 evaluator fix
- **Category**: command
- **Size**: medium (1-2 days)

### 3. Rewind / Partial Summarize ("Summarize up to here")
- **What**: Add a rewind capability that lets the user select a turn boundary and either (a) discard all turns after that point, or (b) summarize all turns before that point while keeping recent turns intact
- **Why**: Claude Code v2.1.141 added "Summarize up to here" in the rewind menu. Our TUI has no rewind capability — once context grows large, the user's only option is full compaction (which summarizes everything) or starting a new session. Partial summarize gives fine-grained control over what the model remembers. The infrastructure already exists: our `compaction/index.ts` supports `preserveRecent` and chunked summarization — we just need a command/UI to trigger it at an arbitrary boundary
- **Source**: anthropics/claude-code v2.1.141 "Rewind menu: added 'Summarize up to here'"
- **Category**: command
- **Size**: medium (1-2 days)

### 4. Startup API Connectivity Timeout
- **What**: Add a 15-second timeout to initial API connectivity/health checks during CLI startup, with a clear user-facing message when the API is unreachable instead of hanging indefinitely
- **Why**: Claude Code v2.1.144 fixed "startup hanging up to 75s when `api.anthropic.com` is unreachable — side-channel API calls now time out after 15s". Our provider layer at `src/providers/` has no explicit timeout guard on startup connectivity. Users behind captive portals, restrictive firewalls, or VPNs may experience indefinite hangs with no feedback. A simple `AbortSignal.timeout(15000)` on startup fetch calls solves this
- **Source**: anthropics/claude-code v2.1.144 "Fixed startup hanging up to 75s when api.anthropic.com is unreachable"
- **Category**: provider
- **Size**: small (< 4h)

### 5. Agent Usage Reset per Execute Call
- **What**: Reset token/cost usage counters at the start of each agent `execute()` call so that per-task usage reporting is accurate and doesn't accumulate across multiple dispatched tasks
- **Why**: Cline v3.0.7 fixed `fix(agents): reset usage at start of each execute() call (#10713)`. If our agent system dispatches multiple tasks (via agent-manager or subagents), accumulated usage from prior tasks bleeds into subsequent task reporting, giving misleading cost/token metrics. This is a simple counter reset at the top of the execution path
- **Source**: cline/cline commit 996a2f6a "fix(agents): reset usage at start of each execute() call"
- **Category**: core
- **Size**: small (< 4h)
